description: Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad cutoff 2025 is finally out for aspirants seeking admission to the BTech and Integrated BTech courses. The JoSAA Round 1 seat allotment result 2025 has been released as opening and closing ranks across different categories belonging to the AI and HS quotas. Candidates can scroll down to this page to view the Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad JEE Main cutoff 2025 for various specialisations and categories. A few more updates are below:

The Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad cutoff 2024 was concluded for JEE Main with the release of the final cutoff list. The cutoff was released as the opening and closing ranks for admission to the BE/BTech, courses. During the Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad BE/BTech cutoff 2024, the last-round closing ranks ranged between 150474 and 327760 for the General AI category students. Of all the BE/BTech specialisations, the one that faced fierce competition was B.Tech. in

The JEE Main cutoff 2024 was released for the General, OBC, ST, SC, EWS, and PWD categories in the AI quota. Considering the last round 2024 cutoff, B.Tech. in Artificial Intelligence(AI) and Data Science was the most sought-after BE/BTech specialization with a closing rank of 150474 for the general AI quota. The closing cutoff for the same stood at 194339 in 2023 and 195763 in 2022. On analysing the cutoff data of three years, B.Tech. in Artificial Intelligence(AI) and Data Science demonstrated

Maharashtra Institute of Technology accepts JEE Main scores, followed by JoSAA and CSAB counselling rounds for admission to BTech and Integrated BTech courses. Candidates seeking admission to Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad can refer to the table below for tentative JEE Main schedule.

Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad JEE Main cutoff 2024 is now over for BTech, Integrated BTech, and BArch courses. For JEE Main, a total of five counselling rounds were conducted. Additionally, two CSAB counselling rounds were also conducted to fill the vacant seats. Candidates can refer to the table below to download the consolidated year-wise counselling rounds for Maharashtra Institute of Technology.

MHT CET is the entrance exam for candidates seeking admission to the BE/BTech and other courses. The cutoff was released across different categories under the All India quota. For the General category, the Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad B.E. / B.Tech cutoff 2025 was 19.45, with the most sought-after branch for admission being Polymer Technology. Refer to the table below to see the first and last-round cutoff percentiles for various B.E. / B.Tech specialisations for General catego

Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad MHT CET cutoff 2025 was released as percentiles, which means the higher the cutoff percentile, the more competitive the course is for admission and vice versa. Considering the last round cutoff data of 2025, Polymer Technology turned out to be the most competitive for admission, with a cutoff closing at 19.45.
Some of the important dates related to MHT CET 2026 for admission are provided below. Candidates can keep track of the upcoming dates to be eligible for admission.
The cutoff PDFs below can be downloaded to view the cutoff details of the last three years for multiple rounds. This will help students understand the past years' cutoff trends.

Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad cutoff 2024 has been released for GATE CCMT. Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad MTech cutoff 2024 is released across various specialisations, among which, M.Tech in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning turned out to be the most competitive specialisation with a cutoff score of 385 for the General AI category students.

The Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad CCMT cutoff 2024 is out for admission to the MTech courses. The toughest course to get into Maharashtra Institute of Technology in the Special Round 1 cutoff list is M.Tech in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning.
Candidates can use the filters below to check out the Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad round, year, and category-wise cutoff scores.
Maharashtra Institute of Technology accepts GATE scores, followed by the CCMT (Centralized Counselling) process for admission to the MTech/ MArch and MPlan courses.
Candidates seeking admission to Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad can refer to the table below for important GATE CCMT dates 2024.

The Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad MAH MBA CET cutoff 2025 is provided in the table below for MBA/PGDM admission, among other courses. The cutoff was released across different categories. For the General AI category, the last-round cutoff percentile for MBA admission was 34.41. For the same course, the cutoff stood at 43.64 in the first round. More details are given below:

Candidates can refer to the table below to view and compare the last three years of MAH MBA CET cutoff data for admission to the MBA course. In 2025, the last round cutoff for MBA admission at Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad was 34.41. For the same course, the last round cutoff was 5.6 in 2024, while in 2023, the cutoff was concluded at 1.43.

Maharashtra Common Entrance Test (MAH CET) is a state level management entrance test for admission to MBA/MMS courses. The MAH MBA CET 2025 cutoff has been released for all rounds and categories. Students can view other important dates for MAH MBA CET 2026 in the table below:

hostel_campus.college_name: Maharashtra Institute of Technology Infrastructure Details & Reviews

reviews.review_heading: My college is good. You can get a nice faculty to communicate with.
reviews.review_text: In the classroom, there is a projector. The projector will be presented through a laptop and it is very easy to understand the concept and logic through the projector. The facility of Wi-Fi connection is the best for students. In the virtual labs, there are computers and a projector. There is a library too and in that library there are so many types of books. The food quality is so good. Every food item is so tasty to eat.

reviews.review_heading: Review by a genuine student of MIT CSN.
reviews.review_text: The campus is large, green, and well-equipped with labs, Wi-Fi, a library, sports facilities, and hostels. That's all.

reviews.review_heading: There is a good learning environment.
reviews.review_text: Some students' behaviour in college is bad. The environment is not very clean. Students are not allowed to sit outside. Some classrooms are poorly maintained with broken lights and switchboards, which need improvement. The campus life here is average. My interaction with my classmates is normal for studying.

reviews.review_heading: Satisfied with the college environment.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure is good enough, and colleges have a big campus surrounded by trees and greenery. Give a positive and good vibe. The hostel and mess are also good, but not that good for students. They have Wi-Fi with a good internet connection. Labs are also average.

reviews.review_heading: It is a good college in all aspects.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of the college is very good. There are two big buildings for this college. The college campus is very big at least 10 to 15 acres. There are Wi-Fi-enabled labs. Although Wi-Fi is not available in the classrooms. The college features a spacious library and well-structured classrooms.

reviews.review_heading: Review of MIT Aurangabad.
reviews.review_text: The college campus has good infrastructure, and the facilities provided to students and faculty are overall good. But sometimes the facilities are poorly managed. Wi-Fi is provided, but only a few can access it. The hostel is good enough for both girls and boys.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Maharashtra Institute of Technology, an autonomous institute, Chatrapati Sambhaji Nagar.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure and facilities are often good. The campus is truly impressive. The campus features modern classrooms equipped with advanced technology. Departments are covered by Wi-Fi. The hostel is average. The mess is also average for students. Also, we have a sports room and various facilities like a cricket ground for tennis, basketball, and volleyball. There is an indoor sports room.

reviews.review_heading: average college in all condition.
reviews.review_text: the facilities provided by the college for mechanical department are not very good as compare to cse department. the quality of the facility of labs for mechanical department not really good. the classrooms or ok. the library of the college is very nice collection of the books is very large. college have decent sports and games not much focuses on sports play in annual function only.

reviews.review_heading: Best college for placements.
reviews.review_text: The hostel of the college is near to the college. It is 5 minutes walk from the college. The hostel infrastructure is very good. The hostel is very clean. There are all facilities in the college and hostel. The quality of food in the mess is really good.

reviews.review_heading: Autonomous college is the best.
reviews.review_text: The next main thing needed is the college facilities. This college provides higher facilities for CSE students. Wi-Fi is provided in labs. We have big classrooms. The college has a library. It has a vast no of collections. The mess quality is good. The college has two separate canteens.

reviews.review_heading: Average college for civil engineering students.
reviews.review_text: The college is big, surrounded by a large area for infrastructure. There is a games club for students in the infrastructure. The college is autonomous and provides all the facilities for students. Facilities like unlimited Wi-Fi access in the practical labs and classrooms are available with advanced technology, and up to 60 students can sit.

reviews.review_heading: Maharashtra Institute of Technology Aurangabad: A Place for Excellence.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of our college is the best. Classrooms are highly advanced with the latest technologies. Labs are equipped with the latest technologies and equipment. There are 2 canteens with a lot of food and drinks on the menu with proper hygiene. Medical facilities are available for students in case of emergencies. High-speed Wi-Fi is available for students.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Maharashtra Institute of Technology, Aurangabad.
reviews.review_text: The college does not have Wi-Fi on campus; it has lab connections instead. I have never stayed in the hostel, so I don't know the quality of the hostel infrastructure, but the college infrastructure is very nice and supports students in their studies.

reviews.review_heading: Mit College of Aurangabad.
reviews.review_text: The hostel is pretty good with proper cleaning, and also the course provided by college is extremely perfect. The hostel is pretty good with proper cleaning, and also the course provided by college is extremely perfect. Also gives the perfect college for students.

reviews.review_heading: MIT -  an autonomous institute of technology.
reviews.review_text: The institute has a large campus with a five-story building and a large playground. There is no Wi-Fi for students, but staff have Wi-Fi access. There are four chemistry labs and four computer labs. The library is open 24 hours a day.

review_summarisation.heading: What students say about Maharashtra Institute of Technology

likes.text: TCS, RedHat, Cognizant, Infosys, Wipro, Tech Mahindra, and many more companies visit our campus for recruitment.

admission_overview.college_name: DKTE Course Admission 2025: Fees, Courses, Eligibility and Selection

admission_process.title: D.K.T.E. Society's Textile and Engineering Institute Admission Process 2025

paragraphs: D.K.T.E. Society's Textile and Engineering Instituteadmission 2025is open for various courses. The institute offers various courses such as BTech, MBA, etc. Apart from these courses, the institute also offers PhD and Diploma courses. The application process atD.K.T.E. Society's Textile and Engineering Instituteis conducted online. The institute also offers scholarships to GATE-qualified students. Read the article to know more aboutD.K.T.E. Society's Textile and Engineering Institutecourse admission2025.

D.K.T.E. Society's Textile and Engineering Institute admission 2025is completely based on the entrance exam as per the respective courses. The university offers more than 10 courses (including specialisations) in various streams such as Engineering, Design, etc. The mode of study of the programme is full-time. Check out the following table for details aboutD.K.T.E. Society's Textile and Engineering Institute course admissions2025:
Particulars
DKTE Admission Highlights 2025
Mode of study
Programmes Offered
Admission status 2025
How to apply?
Level of programmes offered

DKTE course admissionis completely merit-based & entrance exam-based. Students seeking to apply for any of the courses offered at the college must fulfil the admission criteria of the desired programme before applying for it. Post-entrance exam, candidates also have to appear for a counselling session. Students can refer to the table below to know about the eligibility and selection criteria for some of theD.K.T.E. Society's Textile and Engineering Institute courses:
MAT/XAT/CAT/CMAT/ATMA/MAH CET/GMAT
GATE/MHTCET MTech
Merit-based

D.K.T.E. Society's Textile and Engineering Institute admissionsare a mix of entrance and merit-based. Candidates can register online for admission to their preferred course. The admission process at D.K.T.E. Society's Textile and Engineering Institute is as follows:
STEP 1: D.K.T.E. Society's Textile and Engineering Institute Application Process 2025

The application mode is online. Candidates looking for a PhD course application from the affiliated university, i.e., Shivaji University, Kolhapur. Candidates looking for a course application shall approach the office of the respective course institute. Submit the form after receiving the Ph.D. registration confirmation letter from Shivaji University, Kolhapur. PhD Research Scholar (RS) shall apply to the Director, DKTE, for admission to the PhD program from Shivaji University, Kolhapur.

For the rest of course application, candidates must apply at the official website of the respective entrance exam official website. For example, in the BTech course, candidates must first apply for JEE Main or MHT-CET. After qualifying for the entrance exam, candidates must register for the counselling session.
STEP 2: Selection

Candidates must register to take part in the Centralised Admission Process (CAP). In CAP, during counselling, students are required to select their preferred colleges and courses. Based on these preferences and the ranks, seats will be allotted to the candidates. Further, the selection criteria for the PhD course are merit-based.
STEP 3: Merit List & Admission Confirmation

The institute also releases a merit list based on the composite score of the candidates for admission. Shortlisted aspirants need to come for document verification and pay theD.K.T.E. Society's Textile and Engineering Institute feeto confirm their seats at the institute.

reviews.review_heading: The placement ratio is good; the college life is also good.
reviews.review_text: Talking about infrastructure, our college gives students a royal feel. As we enter our classroom, we have good branch whiteboards, and computer labs are good. Talking about the hostel, there is only a girls’ hostel available. Boys’ hostel is available for those who are not from Maharashtra.

reviews.review_heading: Best college in Maharashtra.
reviews.review_text: The best classrooms have a minimum of 70-80 students in one classroom, and there are digital screens fitted for students to study well and understand fully. Washrooms are clean. Hostels are also available in our college. The rooms are also nice.

reviews.review_heading: The average package offered in placements is 4 LPA. Faculty members are supportive and friendly.
reviews.review_text: The college only has a girls' hostel, and boys are living in rooms or PGs, which are near the college and the mess. The cost of the room and mess is affordable. College has specialised departmental libraries for each department and respective labs. Labs are funded by top institutes and MNCs in India.

reviews.review_heading: It is a good college. There are well qualified teachers and the exam pattern is also well managed.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure that our college provides students is a royal fee as we enter college. The classrooms have good branches with whiteboards, and the computer labs are good. There is only a girls hostel available boys hostels are for those who are not from Maharashtra.

reviews.review_heading: The college is really good. Placement is great.
reviews.review_text: Talking about infrastructure, our college gives students a royal feel as we enter college, the classrooms have good branches with whiteboards, and the computer labs are good. Talking about the hostel, there is only girls hostel available and boys hostel is for those who are not from Maharashtra.

reviews.review_heading: Good placement, friendly seniors, good crowd, and royalty.
reviews.review_text: Talking about infrastructure, our college gives students a royal feel. As we enter college, the classrooms have good branches with whiteboards, and the computer labs are good. Talking about the hostel, there is only a girls' hostel available, and the boys' hostel is for those who are not from Maharashtra.

reviews.review_heading: Supportive faculty, modern campus, decent opportunities.
reviews.review_text: All facilities are available. They are helpful for any academic problem, and they teach very well. In the college, there is good drinking water, the best food, and hostel facilities are good. It is safe and good, and  Wi-Fi is available.

reviews.review_heading: The important thing is to choose college wisely.
reviews.review_text: The Wi-Fi and labs are in average condition. The hostel, course, and classroom are in average conditions so we cannot study properly and concentrate properly on study. The canteen is not hygienic and improving the quality issue so students can eat there. Medicines are also available.

reviews.review_heading: The proper college of engineering.
reviews.review_text: There is continued internet connectivity. There are separate labs for every new subject, and classrooms are well-maintained. The quality of food available in the hostel is good. It feels like home. The food quality is well maintained in mess, canteen, and mini canteen.

reviews.review_heading: The improper college has no facilities and services.
reviews.review_text: Facilities are average but not excellent. College mess and hostels are expensive and have too low facilities, with no Wi-Fi and toilets. Lab and classrooms are not connected to Wi-Fi and the library is under construction so we can't study properly in college so we are busy in canteen.

reviews.review_heading: DKTE college student feedback.
reviews.review_text: My college has facilities, water purifiers are available everywhere on campus. It has the best campus with a good environment. We have well-built lab facilities and well-cleaned classrooms. The library has all the books for every course or subject. The canteen is good, and the food is best.

reviews.review_heading: Honest Review of DKTE: Straightforward and Unbiased.
reviews.review_text: DKTE has well-equipped labs for the Textile Department. The labs have machinery for testing, spinning, weaving, and dyeing. Classrooms are also quite spacious and equipped with projectors and speakers for digital learning. Hostels are only available for girls, and they are inside the campus. Canteen and mess facilities are available, but the food quality is not that good.

reviews.review_heading: Great infrastructure and good placement support.
reviews.review_text: DKTE Ichalkaranji offers advanced labs, Wi-Fi-enabled smart classrooms, a vast library and modern infrastructure. Hostels provide clean rooms, good food in the mess or canteen, medical aid and excellent sports facilities for indoor and outdoor games.

reviews.review_heading: It is good for textile students.
reviews.review_text: College infrastructure is average, and there is a library with Wi-Fi. Labs are great. Campus is not very large, but it is decent and has a canteen with a lot of delicious food. There is a sports department that includes a gym, table tennis, and many other facilities.

reviews.review_heading: College has good infrastructure and supportive staff. Active mentors. Exams are taken very strictly.
reviews.review_text: College labs are well-equipped and have fastest internet connections, about 100 mb/s. Classrooms are also well maintained. There is a projector in every classroom. Some have digital screens. Canteen food quality is average, not so good. For sports, there are two gyms. The first is for boys, which is not well-equipped, and the second is for girls, which has advanced equipment.

college_info.college_name: KIIT School of Fashion Technology - KSFT

highlights.summary: KIIT School of Fashion Technology - KSFT was founded in 2009. It is a private institute and is located in Bhubaneswar, Orissa. KIIT School of Fashion Technology - KSFT courses are offered to students at the UG, PG and Doctorate levels. The institute is approved by AICTE and offers courses in Full Time mode. KIIT School of Fashion Technology - KSFT offers a total of 4 courses in streams such as Business & Management Studies and Design. Some of the popular courses offered at the institute are B.Des, Ph.D. and M.A. and the total seat intake for the available courses is 120. These courses are offered to students across several specialisations such as Fashion Design, Fashion Management and Textile Design. Further, the fee for all these courses ranges between INR 7,20,000 to 7,68,000. Apart from this, the KIIT School of Fashion Technology - KSFT campus has a state-of-the-art infrastructure with all the advanced facilities.

placements.overview: KIIT School of Fashion Technology - KSFT was founded in 2009. It is a private institute and is located in Bhubaneswar, Orissa. KIIT School of Fashion Technology - KSFT courses are offered to students at the UG, PG and Doctorate levels. The institute is approved by AICTE and offers courses in Full Time mode. KIIT School of Fashion Technology - KSFT offers a total of 4 courses in streams such as Business & Management Studies and Design. Some of the popular courses offered at the institute are B.Des, Ph.D. and M.A. and the total seat intake for the available courses is 120. These courses are offered to students across several specialisations such as Fashion Design, Fashion Management and Textile Design. Further, the fee for all these courses ranges between INR 7,20,000 to 7,68,000. Apart from this, the KIIT School of Fashion Technology - KSFT campus has a state-of-the-art infrastructure with all the advanced facilities.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Des. in Fashion - Batch of 2025
reviews_list.review_title: Faculty is really helpful and supportive!

review_content.Placements: College placement is good, 70% of students are placed each year. The highest package offered is 8 LPA and the lowest package offered 2.5 LPA. 90% of students are offered summer internship in good fashion houses, eg. Torani's, Aditya Birla, lifestyle etc. Roles offered are usually assistant designers.
review_content.Infrastructure: Infrastructure is not so good, Wi-Fi is not helpful at all. Library is average, but it have all the documentation by top students over the years and reference for many projects. Hostel facilities are really good but mess food is inhumane. Security is good and tight.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Des. in Fashion - Batch of 2025
reviews_list.review_title: Very good faculty and education system.

review_content.Placements: Many different workshops are held. Top recruiting companies are Birla, Pantaloons, and many other brands. Everyone here gets an internship based on their work. An internship is offered on the basis of performance. Many students get admission each year to the Kiit School of Fashion Technology, which is one of the best fashion colleges in Odisha.
review_content.Infrastructure: The facilities are very good. There is a library especially for fashion students. Wi-Fi is very good for all the students. The quality of the food is also very good. Different playgrounds are available to people for different sports. There is also a gym room for students.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Des. in Fashion - Batch of 2022
reviews_list.review_title: The college is good but not the best as compared to other government colleges.

review_content.Placements: Almost 70% of the students are placed. The highest salary package offered to the textile student was 10 LPA. The lowest salary package offered is 2.5 LPA, and the average salary package offered is 3-4 LPA. Aditya Birla Group visited our campus for placements. Roles offered were fashion and textile design. In our course, the highest salary package offered is 10 LPA, the average salary package offered is 3-4 LPA, and minimum salary package offered is 2 LPA. Almost 20-30% of the students got an internship in the Aditya Birla Group.
review_content.Infrastructure: All the facilities are available nowadays in our college and day by day resources are provided conveniently to us. Notes in the form of PDF are also provided, and smart classrooms are always available for virtual understanding. Hostel facilities are far better than other colleges in India. It is value for money and never make us feel like we are far away from home.

highlights.summary: FAD Institute of Luxury Fashion & Style, is located in Mumbai, Maharashtra. FAD Institute of Luxury Fashion & Style provides 14 UG, PG and Certificate courses in 4 specialisations, namely Fashion Design, Brand Management, Fashion Management and Makeup & Beautician. Theses programmes are offered in both Full Time and Part Time modes. B.A., UG Diploma, M.A. and Certificate are some of the courses offered at FAD Institute of Luxury Fashion & Style. Some of the streams in which these courses are offered include Business & Management Studies, Arts ( Fine / Visual / Performing ), Design and Beauty & Fitness. FAD Institute of Luxury Fashion & Style offers all the infrastructural facilities to students.

A:    SASTRA (Deemed to be University) and School of Science and Humanities, SRM IST, Chennai both are renowned universities for B.Sc. However, in terms of affordability, SASTRA (Deemed to be University) is a better option since the total tuition fee for SASTRA (Deemed to be University) is INR 200,000 and the total tuition fee for B.Sc. at School of Science and Humanities, SRM IST, Chennai is INR 75,000 - INR 380,000. 
In the total fees that students generally end up paying the university or the college, various other components are also included, such as the exam fee, application fee, lab and other charges.

reviews_list.batch_info: BCA in Data Science - Batch of 2026
reviews_list.review_title: It has a good campus to explore. College life is amazing.

review_content.Placements: I am from the 2023-2026 BCA data science batch at SRM Kattankulathur, and honestly, placements have been pretty decent for our course. Around 80-85% of students got placed last year. The highest package I heard is 8 LPA, and the lowest package is 3 LPA. The average package is 4.5 LPA. Big companies like Infosys, Cognizant, TCS, Capgemini, and Wipro visited the college for placements. Also, quite a few students got internships, i.e., around 70% in companies like Zoho, Virtusa, and HCL, and some even through a coding platform.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure here is honestly top-notch. We have good labs, strong Wi-Fi in academic buildings, clean classrooms with projectors, and a huge library. There are plenty of systems for practicing coding. The campus is fully digital and pretty convenient. Hostel life is good too. I stay in the non-AC block, and it is comfortable and clean.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com.) - Batch of 2026
reviews_list.review_title: Faculty members of science and humanities - B.Com at SRM.

review_content.Placements: In my course B.Com, almost 80% of students are getting placed every year. Students who are interested in banking jobs are highly likely to get a job as most of the companies that visit are banking institutions. Right after finishing B.Com, the highest package will be 3 LPA - 4 LPA. Internships are not exactly provided by the placement office in our college. Students have to search for internships on their own and the college will just approve it. Top roles offered in B.Com are analyst, accountant, sales analyst, etc.
review_content.Infrastructure: Compared to the infrastructure of the engineering departments, the classrooms of B.Com are average. Free Wi-Fi is available to all the students. Central library at SRM is the biggest asset of the college. Any student, faculty member, or research scholar can have access to the books present in the library. It is huge and massive and has books of all genres and related courses. The food in the canteens is top-notch. There are so many varieties of foods available for students of all sorts of ethnicity and the population is diverse.

faqs.answer: Humanities as a stream provides a wide range of courses that can be studied after completing their 12th grade. Pursuing any of these degrees prepares you to understand the various levels of humanities and, as a result, carve out an appropriate job path. Candidates in the humanities can apply for positions in the government sector, such as banking firms, government ministries, legal firms, and political analysts.Content Writer, Civil Services, Human Resource Manager, Defense Services, Lawyer, Journalist, Counsellor, and Therapist positions are available. As freshers, humanities graduates can expect to earn between INR 2-6 LPA.
faqs.question: Q: What is the Scope of Humanities and Social Science courses?

faqs.answer: The best choice depends on your career aspirations:· If you want to become a teacher: Opt for a B.Ed. It's the mandatory qualification for teaching in most schools.· If you want to pursue a career in research or a science-related field: A BSc would be more suitable. It provides a strong foundation for further studies and specialised careers.· If you're unsure: Consider your interests and skills. Do you enjoy teaching and interacting with students? Or are you more drawn to scientific exploration and research? Additional options:· M.Ed.: If you already have a B.Ed., you can pursue a Master of Education for career advancement or specialisation in a particular teaching area.· Diploma courses: Various specialised Diploma courses in education or related fields can enhance your skills and knowledge.
faqs.question: Q: Which courses can I do after a D.Ed.? Should I do a BSc or B.Ed?

faqs.answer: The last date for submission of the SRMJEEE 2024 application form was June 15, 2024. The SRMJEEE is the entrance exam for admission into a BTech and MTech course at SRM University Chennai. The university offers over 80 UG, PG, doctoral, and diploma courses. Admission for most of the courses is on an entrance basis, and university admission considers national-level entrance examinations such as CAT, MAT, XAT, TANCET, and NATA.
faqs.question: Q: What is the last date to apply for School of Science and Humanities SRM admission?

faqs.answer: To gain entry into the M.Sc. degree programs offered at the School of Science and Humanities, SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST), the university mostly accepts a valid SRM Joint Entrance Examination (SRMJEEH) score. This examination takes place for a number of postgraduate programs including those under the Science and Humanities faculties. Moreover, some courses may have their own entrance components, like the SRM Future Skills Test for some specializations such as Applied Data Science.

overview_faqs.question: Is School of Science and Humanities, SRM IST, Chennai good for BSc?
overview_faqs.answer: School of Science and Humanities at SRM IST, Chennai is considered good choice for BSc offering research oriented, industry ready curriculum with solid placement support especially for top performers. It has a strong infrastructure.

review_summary.college_name: SRM School of Science and Humanities Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: B.Sc. in Defence and Strategic Studies - Batch of 2027
individual_reviews.title: This provides a comprehensive, honest insight into campus life, academics, and opportunities.

content.Placements: Most recruitment is in GCL and Texco. Internships are offered in cybercrime, ethical hacking, and criminal law.
content.Infrastructure: There is a small section of books in the central library. Classrooms are very good, and there is an excellent infrastructure in the department. The hostel is very nice with a neat and clean mess, bathrooms, and rooms, along with top-level security in the hostel. The quality of food is very good.
content.Faculty: Teachers are very knowledgeable and well-experienced. They are also very friendly and supportive. This course is very helpful for students who are preparing for CDS, SSB, UPSC, and other competitive exams. There are six semester exams in three years, and the pass percentage is 95%.

individual_reviews.course: B.A. in Journalism and Mass Communication - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: B.A. JMC at SRM Kattankulatur.

content.Placements: Weekly alumni talks help students build connections with people in the industry. Most students graduate and get placed through referral. Packages vary from 3 LPA - 7 LPA again depending on campus placements or referral. Recruiting companies are The Hindu, TOI, and Zoho. Roles offered are journalists, editors, HR, PR, and communication roles.
content.Infrastructure: Classrooms have projectors, but ventilation can be a problem because there are no air-conditioners in this block. Labs have PCs, cameras, and microphones for recording and inbuilt editing software. Hostel food is good and hygienic, but in time is strict, and that can pose an issue.

individual_reviews.course: BCA in Data Science - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: It has a good campus to explore. College life is amazing.

content.Placements: I am from the 2023-2026 BCA data science batch at SRM Kattankulathur, and honestly, placements have been pretty decent for our course. Around 80-85% of students got placed last year. The highest package I heard is 8 LPA, and the lowest package is 3 LPA. The average package is 4.5 LPA. Big companies like Infosys, Cognizant, TCS, Capgemini, and Wipro visited the college for placements. Also, quite a few students got internships, i.e., around 70% in companies like Zoho, Virtusa, and HCL, and some even through a coding platform.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure here is honestly top-notch. We have good labs, strong Wi-Fi in academic buildings, clean classrooms with projectors, and a huge library. There are plenty of systems for practicing coding. The campus is fully digital and pretty convenient. Hostel life is good too. I stay in the non-AC block, and it is comfortable and clean.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com.) - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: Faculty members of science and humanities - B.Com at SRM.

content.Placements: In my course B.Com, almost 80% of students are getting placed every year. Students who are interested in banking jobs are highly likely to get a job as most of the companies that visit are banking institutions. Right after finishing B.Com, the highest package will be 3 LPA - 4 LPA. Internships are not exactly provided by the placement office in our college. Students have to search for internships on their own and the college will just approve it. Top roles offered in B.Com are analyst, accountant, sales analyst, etc.
content.Infrastructure: Compared to the infrastructure of the engineering departments, the classrooms of B.Com are average. Free Wi-Fi is available to all the students. Central library at SRM is the biggest asset of the college. Any student, faculty member, or research scholar can have access to the books present in the library. It is huge and massive and has books of all genres and related courses. The food in the canteens is top-notch. There are so many varieties of foods available for students of all sorts of ethnicity and the population is diverse.

individual_reviews.course: M.Sc. in Applied Data Science - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: Great college, but it totally depends on how you want to shape your life for good or bad.

content.Placements: SRM has decent placements, with about 80-90% of students getting placed. For 2024, the highest package was 52 LPA, while the average for most students was around 4-7 LPA, depending on the program. Top companies like Microsoft, Google, Amazon, IBM, and TCS recruit for roles like Data Scientist, Analyst, and Machine Learning Engineer. Many students also get internships in companies like Deloitte and Infosys, making the Data Science program a good choice due to the demand for skilled professionals.
content.Infrastructure: Facilities and infrastructure are good, but you don’t have enough time to complete all the programs within the lab hours themselves. You have to work on your own laptop to complete it all and learn a bit. Wi-Fi is really good; you can download a 1GB movie within 5 minutes. As for classrooms, they’re average for the FSH building. Hostel facilities are good and totally depend on the amount you pay for a better hostel. The food is average, like in any other college. You will get free consultations and 50% off on medical tests if you are an SRM student.

faqs.question: What is the last date to apply for School of Science and Humanities SRM admission?
faqs.answer: The last date for submission of the SRMJEEE 2024 application form was June 15, 2024. The SRMJEEE is the entrance exam for admission into a BTech and MTech course at SRM University Chennai.The university offers over 80 UG, PG, doctoral, and diploma courses. Admission for most of the courses is on an entrance basis, and university admission considers national-level entrance examinations such as CAT, MAT, XAT, TANCET, and NATA.

faqs.question: What entrance exam scores are accepted by the School of Science and Humanities SRM for admission to M.Sc.?
faqs.answer: To gain entry into the M.Sc. degree programs offered at the School of Science and Humanities, SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST), the university mostly accepts a valid SRM Joint Entrance Examination (SRMJEEH) score. This examination takes place for a number of postgraduate programs including those under the Science and Humanities faculties. Moreover, some courses may have their own entrance components, like the SRM Future Skills Test for some specializations such as Applied Data Science.

faqs.question: Is it easy to get admission in School of Science and Humanities, SRM Kattankulathur?
faqs.answer: Admission to School of Science and Humanities at SRM IST Kattankulathur is generally considered moderately easy for most undergraduate programs often based on merit in 10+2 examinations although it is highly reputed institution. While competitive for top courses direct admission is available for high performers & management quota seats are also an option.

faqs.question: How to get admission in School of Science and Humanities at SRM IST, Chennai?
faqs.answer: Admission to School of Science and Humanities at SRM IST Chennai for 2026 is primarily based on SRM Joint Entrance Examination for PG courses while UG admissions often consider merit in qualifying exams. Application process is online with fee per phase. Direct admission is possible for high performing students, board toppers & athletes.

reviews.review_heading: Great facilities and supportive faculty at SRM.

reviews.review_heading: Enough infrastructure and good faculty.

reviews.review_heading: Everything is good at this college.

reviews.review_heading: Value for money and satisfied with the faculty members.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Des. in Digital Product Design/UI and UX (Industry Integrated) - Batch of 2029
reviews_list.review_title: It is a good college for students who are seeking a design course.

review_content.Placements: Courses, UI/UX, and most design courses are popular in Imaginexp, which also helps with placements. Industry integration helps students learn better through projects and assignments. It allows them to understand concepts more deeply.
review_content.Infrastructure: Classrooms are clean. Hostel rooms are good with Wi-Fi, though there is no Wi-Fi in classrooms. Clean food is provided in the mess and canteens. Vending machines are available for late-night cravings. There is a 24/7 water and electricity supply for uninterrupted study sessions. Laundry facilities are available, and washing machines are also provided in the hostel.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Des. in Graphic Design - Batch of 2025
reviews_list.review_title: Absolutely dissatisfied because of management, fees, academics and infrastructure .

review_content.Placements: Our scenes are finding internships on there own. Only one student was in his 4th year; even he did not get any placement. Opportunities are not much here; we have to find and apply for internships on our own; we only get a referral later and nothing else. I do not know much about the internship process; last year, we had 6 months to do internships and the rest of the time to submit our thesis.
review_content.Infrastructure: Hostelers complain about food quality and hygiene. I don't use hostel facilities, but my batchmates are in hospitals. For an outing, you have to write an application even if you want to go outside of the gate just to hang out with your friends during break time.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Architecture (B.Arch.) - Batch of 2026
reviews_list.review_title: Dissatisfied with placements for architecture course.

review_content.Placements: Architecture is a newly added course. My seniors have acquired good jobs in big firms, but on their own. I am hoping that the college will provide placements in future. Two batches have already passed and the present batch is also not getting any placement assistance, except for 3 students. At this pace, I don't think so they'll give us placement opportunities.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure and hostels are moderately good. There are not much softwares available in computer labs, only a few like rhino, revit. No computer and staffs are available for teaching the software. We figured out, how to use the software in college PCs, on our own. The classrooms and libraries are quite good.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Des. in Graphic Design - Batch of 2024
reviews_list.review_title: The worst college I have ever seen.

review_content.Placements: Students were not placed till now, and no one received any job package. Companies not visited for graphic design roles. Only 5–6 companies came overall, and none provided relevant or meaningful placement opportunities. This college was just a money-making machine.
review_content.Infrastructure: The faculty provided little to no valuable guidance, making learning ineffective. The infrastructure was still incomplete after years, creating a poor environment. Wi-Fi connectivity was extremely unreliable. The hostel food was of very low quality and often inedible.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Architecture (B.Arch.) - Batch of 2023
reviews_list.review_title: Good studies good infrastructure .

review_content.Placements: Placements are not done in our course But college placement are good packages available. Good infrastructure. But yet studies are good. I have nothing else to say because my course doesn't get a placement. Why should I write 250 words for this.
review_content.Infrastructure: Free Wi-Fi 24 hours water electricity 3 floors. Good design open spaces available. Playgrounds available open cafe and canteen good ventilation. Views from room are good. Not much distance from college. Feasible to use. 24*7 Warden abailbe.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Des. in Digital Product Design/UI and UX (Industry Integrated) - Batch of 2027
reviews_list.review_title: Satisfied with faculty members, bad management, average infrastructure, and facilities.

review_content.Placements: There are 17 students in my batch. This is the first batch of this course so no one has been placed yet in my course. They have provided an internship (online) which is for 45 days to all the students. Companies like Infosys, and IBM come here for placement.
review_content.Infrastructure: We've our class with benches, whiteboards, and a podium. There's no Wi-Fi. We work on our laptops so we don't need to go to the lab. We can visit the library whenever we want and books are available most of the time. Hostel rooms are quite good, they have provided beds, a study table, chairs, and an almirah and have a separate bathroom. There are many Wi-Fi. The food provided in the mess is not satisfying, quality is not good. Many times we've found insects in food and sometimes the food gets finished before time. The warden's behavior is not so good.

review_summary.college_name: Dev Bhoomi School of Architecture and Design Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: B.Des. in Digital Product Design/UI and UX (Industry Integrated) - Batch of 2029
individual_reviews.title: It is a good college for students who are seeking a design course.

content.Placements: Courses, UI/UX, and most design courses are popular in Imaginexp, which also helps with placements. Industry integration helps students learn better through projects and assignments. It allows them to understand concepts more deeply.
content.Infrastructure: Classrooms are clean. Hostel rooms are good with Wi-Fi, though there is no Wi-Fi in classrooms. Clean food is provided in the mess and canteens. Vending machines are available for late-night cravings. There is a 24/7 water and electricity supply for uninterrupted study sessions. Laundry facilities are available, and washing machines are also provided in the hostel.

individual_reviews.course: B.Des. in Graphic Design - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: Absolutely dissatisfied because of management, fees, academics and infrastructure .

content.Placements: Our scenes are finding internships on there own. Only one student was in his 4th year; even he did not get any placement. Opportunities are not much here; we have to find and apply for internships on our own; we only get a referral later and nothing else. I do not know much about the internship process; last year, we had 6 months to do internships and the rest of the time to submit our thesis.
content.Infrastructure: Hostelers complain about food quality and hygiene. I don't use hostel facilities, but my batchmates are in hospitals. For an outing, you have to write an application even if you want to go outside of the gate just to hang out with your friends during break time.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Architecture (B.Arch.) - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: Dissatisfied with placements for architecture course.

content.Placements: Architecture is a newly added course. My seniors have acquired good jobs in big firms, but on their own. I am hoping that the college will provide placements in future. Two batches have already passed and the present batch is also not getting any placement assistance, except for 3 students. At this pace, I don't think so they'll give us placement opportunities.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure and hostels are moderately good. There are not much softwares available in computer labs, only a few like rhino, revit. No computer and staffs are available for teaching the software. We figured out, how to use the software in college PCs, on our own. The classrooms and libraries are quite good.

individual_reviews.course: B.Des. in Graphic Design - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: The worst college I have ever seen.

content.Placements: Students were not placed till now, and no one received any job package. Companies not visited for graphic design roles. Only 5–6 companies came overall, and none provided relevant or meaningful placement opportunities. This college was just a money-making machine.
content.Infrastructure: The faculty provided little to no valuable guidance, making learning ineffective. The infrastructure was still incomplete after years, creating a poor environment. Wi-Fi connectivity was extremely unreliable. The hostel food was of very low quality and often inedible.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Architecture (B.Arch.) - Batch of 2023
individual_reviews.title: Good studies good infrastructure .

content.Placements: Placements are not done in our course But college placement are good packages available. Good infrastructure. But yet studies are good. I have nothing else to say because my course doesn't get a placement. Why should I write 250 words for this.
content.Infrastructure: Free Wi-Fi 24 hours water electricity 3 floors. Good design open spaces available. Playgrounds available open cafe and canteen good ventilation. Views from room are good. Not much distance from college. Feasible to use. 24*7 Warden abailbe.

admission_process.title: Dev Bhoomi School of Architecture, Planning and Design Admission Process 2026

paragraphs: Dev Bhoomi School of Architecture, Planning and Design admission processinvolves application, selection, and admission confirmation. The process can be completed online or offline. After the admission is confirmed, candidates need to deposit the admission fee as per college norms. The steps forDev Bhoomi School of Architecture, Planning and Design admissionare as below:
Application Process 2026

The mode of application is both online & offline. For online application, students can register at the university's website by paying application fee and submitting online form. For offline application, students need to walk-in at the university campus and collect the application form and prospectus offline.
Selection Process 2026

At Dev Bhoomi School of Architecture, Planning and Design, students are selected either based on academic merit or entrance test scores depending on the course. Students also need to go through a university-level counselling as part of the process.
Admission confirmation 2026
Once the selection rounds are done, the shortlisted candidates are called for verification of documents. Aspirants have to pay a course fee to confirm their seat in the university.

Dev Bhoomi School of Architecture, Planning and Design fee structureconsists of tuition fee, library fee, caution deposit, admission fee, university enrollment fee, uniform fee, etc. Some of these are charged only once. The remaining amount is to be paid either yearly or semester-wise. hostel_campus.college_name: Dev Bhoomi School of Architecture and Design Infrastructure Details & Reviews

reviews.review_heading: It is a good college for students who are seeking a design course.
reviews.review_text: Classrooms are clean. Hostel rooms are good with Wi-Fi, though there is no Wi-Fi in classrooms. Clean food is provided in the mess and canteens. Vending machines are available for late-night cravings. There is a 24/7 water and electricity supply for uninterrupted study sessions. Laundry facilities are available, and washing machines are also provided in the hostel.

reviews.review_heading: Absolutely dissatisfied because of management, fees, academics and infrastructure .
reviews.review_text: Hostelers complain about food quality and hygiene. I don't use hostel facilities, but my batchmates are in hospitals. For an outing, you have to write an application even if you want to go outside of the gate just to hang out with your friends during break time.

reviews.review_heading: Dissatisfied with placements for architecture course.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure and hostels are moderately good. There are not much softwares available in computer labs, only a few like rhino, revit. No computer and staffs are available for teaching the software. We figured out, how to use the software in college PCs, on our own. The classrooms and libraries are quite good.

reviews.review_heading: The worst college I have ever seen.
reviews.review_text: The faculty provided little to no valuable guidance, making learning ineffective. The infrastructure was still incomplete after years, creating a poor environment. Wi-Fi connectivity was extremely unreliable. The hostel food was of very low quality and often inedible.

reviews.review_heading: Good studies good infrastructure .
reviews.review_text: Free Wi-Fi 24 hours water electricity 3 floors. Good design open spaces available. Playgrounds available open cafe and canteen good ventilation. Views from room are good. Not much distance from college. Feasible to use. 24*7 Warden abailbe.

reviews.review_heading: Satisfied with faculty members, bad management, average infrastructure, and facilities.
reviews.review_text: We've our class with benches, whiteboards, and a podium. There's no Wi-Fi. We work on our laptops so we don't need to go to the lab. We can visit the library whenever we want and books are available most of the time. Hostel rooms are quite good, they have provided beds, a study table, chairs, and an almirah and have a separate bathroom. There are many Wi-Fi. The food provided in the mess is not satisfying, quality is not good. Many times we've found insects in food and sometimes the food gets fi...

paragraphs: Dev Bhoomi School of Architecture and Design total fees, includes multiple components like tuition fee, hostel fees, mess charges, one time charges and others. The criteria for selection for scholarships at Dev Bhoomi School of Architecture and Design BSc programme includes:
Diversity Scholarship Policy: A scholarship of up to 10% on first-year academic fees will be awarded to students from any state in India.

Uttarakhand State Domicile Policy: Students with a permanent address (DOMICILE) in Uttarakhand or those who have completed their Class 10 or 12 education in Uttarakhand are eligible for a scholarship of up to 25% per year on tuition fees.

Other Hilly State Domicile Policy: A scholarship of up to 10% per year on academic fees is offered to support meritorious and talented students from Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Sikkim, Nagaland, Assam, Tripura, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Mizoram, Meghalaya, Himachal Pradesh and District Darjeeling, Kalimpong (West Bengal).
Meritorious Scholarships: Scholarships are granted to students based on the merit they demonstrate in their qualifying examinations. For better understanding refer to the table mentioned below:
